Brent — the global benchmark with a physical overlay¶

Sample: 2019-04-16 → 2026-04-17 · 1 809 trading days · ICE Brent M1-M12 (BRN 1!-ICE … BRN 12!-ICE) + Platts Dated Brent DTD (2026-01-02 → 2026-04-14, 69 days) for the physical-paper basis study.

What Brent is in the market: the global waterborne crude benchmark. Every non-Americas crude differential prices to it (Urals, Forties, WAF grades, some Middle-East formulas). When Hormuz is blocked, when sanctions reshape flows, when OPEC cuts — Brent moves first. For a trading desk, Brent is the instrument you can trade; Dated Brent is the physical assessment you reference to mark your book.

Sample-length caveat — up front, not in a footer. In-sample window ends 2024-12-31; OOS is ≈16 months (≈333 trading days). All regime-frequency claims below carry wide error bars at this sample length. Direction is unambiguous; level is descriptive of the current episode, not predictive of a distribution.

What this notebook argues:

  1. Backwardation default, same structural finding as LSGO and WTI. 68.9% of days M1 > M3 — oil storage economics work against carry.
  2. The 2026 OOS is bimodal: either Normal (82%) or Crisis (17%), with Tight/Stress nearly empty. Brent prices geopolitical premium discontinuously — the market doesn't gradually walk from tight to stress to crisis, it jumps. That discontinuity is what paper-only risk models miss.
  3. The April 2026 Dated-vs-paper dislocation is the defining physical-paper event. Dated printed 144.42 USD/bbl on 2026-04-07 while Jun-26 futures settled at 109.27 — a ≈ $35 cash-over-paper premium. That's not a curve anomaly; it's a physical prompt scarcity that the paper market structurally cannot bid for.
  4. The long-history OPEC-ASB chart puts today's level in 36-year context (Section 6). Not a trading tool — a sanity check against recency bias.

Structured in six sections: (1) term structure + M1-M12 full strip; (2) anomaly detection and distribution; (3) events, dashboard, CDF regime label; (4) physical-paper event study on the Dated file; (5) HMM probabilistic regime + next-day forecast; (6) 36y OPEC long-history baseline.

1. Term structure — price, vol, full M1-M12 strip¶

Flat price + 20-day realised vol on top, daily M1-M12 forward curves (regime-coloured) beneath, and the short-dated spreads as standalone time series. Brent's term structure is more forgiving than LSGO's — narrower magnitudes, more frequent contango windows (2020 COVID, 2023 OPEC cuts post-announcement). The 2022 and 2026 backwardation prints are real but never reach LSGO's pp-of-crude scale.

In [6]:
fca.chart_price_vol();
In [7]:
fca.chart_forward_curves();
In [8]:
fca.chart_short_structure();
In [9]:
fca.chart_long_structure();
In [10]:
fca.chart_full_strip();

2. Anomaly detection — IS-anchored Z-scores and the distribution¶

Two Z-score panels (M1-M3, M1-M6) using the pre-2025 mean and std. The ±5 visual cap is for readability; peak values are annotated. Then the distribution panel — IS (near-Gaussian, excess kurtosis 79) vs OOS (extreme-tailed, bimodal). The bimodality is the key Brent-specific feature: prints sit either in Normal or in Crisis, not on a continuum.

In [11]:
fca.chart_zscore(SPREAD='M1_M3');
In [12]:
fca.chart_zscore(SPREAD='M1_M6');
In [13]:
fca.chart_distribution();

3. Events, dashboard, CDF regime label¶

Portfolio-wide event overlay on the spread series; 4-panel dashboard for a single-screen read; CDF regime classification using 80/95/99 percentiles on pre-2025 M1-M6 (Tight/Stress/Crisis thresholds 1.07 / 1.33 / 1.51 USD/bbl).

In [14]:
fca.chart_event_timeline();
In [15]:
fca.chart_dashboard();
In [16]:
fca.chart_regime();

4. Dated Brent vs paper M1 — April 2026 event study¶

Data window is short. The Dated assessment file covers only 2026-01-02 → 2026-04-14 (69 days, 3.5 months) — nothing further back. Treat the chart as an event study around the April 7 dislocation, not a long-run basis analysis. Dated printed 144.42 USD/bbl on 2026-04-07 while Jun-26 futures settled at 109.27 — a ≈ $35 physical-paper gap. That gap is the cash-over-paper basis trade: long physical, short paper swap, bank the premium as paper catches up (the mirror image of the Med Cargo Week-15 products basis trade).

In [17]:
fca.chart_dated_event_study(EVENT_DATE='2026-04-07');

5. HMM probabilistic regime + next-day forecast¶

The CDF classifier gives hard labels; the HMM gives probabilistic state labels from a 2-state Gaussian emission model plus a transition matrix — useful for boundary prints where a single M1-M6 reading sits on the Crisis threshold. Fit is on the full sample because the pre-2025 distribution is essentially unimodal (no Crisis emission to estimate from IS alone). The next-day forecast iterates today's state vector through the transition matrix for +1 to +5 days.

In [18]:
fca.chart_hmm_regime(SPREAD='M1_M6', N_STATES=2);

HMM means (by state): [ 0.4121642  12.90464468]
HMM std   (by state): [0.61629259 7.06069064]
Transition matrix:
[[9.99434043e-01 5.65957128e-04]
 [1.54333383e-18 1.00000000e+00]]
IS  P(crisis)>0.5 days: 0/1476 (0.0%)
OOS P(crisis)>0.5 days: 42/333 (12.6%)

fca.forecast_next_day_regime(HORIZON=5);

6. Long-history context — where does today sit vs 36 years?¶

OPEC's Annual Statistical Bulletin (Table 74) publishes monthly front-month Brent and WTI settlements from 1990 onwards. Monthly, not daily, and it doesn't feed the engine — it's a sanity check against recency bias: does today's $95-100 level actually rank as extreme in a 36-year window? The shaded IS/OOS windows match our 2024-12-31 calibration cut, and four canonical events are annotated (Gulf War 1990, GFC peak 2008, pre-shale-crash 2014, Russia invasion 2022) so the reader can triangulate against real episodes, not just percentile ranks.

In [20]:
fca.chart_long_history_context();

Brent monthly — 36y context
   Peak   : 134.6 on 2008-07
   Trough : 10.3 on 1998-12
   Latest : 92.3 on 2010-12 (rank 85th pct of 36y sample)

Trader takeaways¶

  • Storage-flexible and moderate in structure. Backwardation 68.9% with small magnitudes, contango accessible in calm windows. Brent is not the curve-instability benchmark — that's LSGO.
  • Bimodal OOS is the signature. The Normal ↔ Crisis jump during geopolitical events is the hardest thing for paper-only models to handle. Size position risk for discontinuous moves, not gradual regime drift.
  • Physical-paper basis is the April 2026 story. Dated − M1 reached ~$35 at peak. A long-physical / short-paper basis trade banked that premium cleanly — the same structure that the Med Cargo Market Week-15 desk trade #5 describes on products.
  • Prompt is where the action lives. Corr(M1-M3, Vol) > Corr(M1-M6, Vol). Geopolitical headlines hit the front first; the back is where curve-flattening trades set up once the event has peaked.
  • 36-year context: today ranks top-quartile but not all-time. $95-100 Brent was common in 2008 and 2011-2014. The 2026 print is elevated, not unprecedented — keep the recency bias in check.
